title: Keyboard accepts physical keys only and cleans held state

preconditions.description: The harness holds the inherited device-keyed lease before any device or fixture access.
The harness recorded session generation, display, layout, lock state, ATX-observable state, browser identity, and fixture identity.
A current observation exists and the keyboard plane resolves only the canonical physical-key allowlist.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot cancellation for the next admitted keyboard request immediately before its first BrowserPlane write.
fault_script.expected_effect: The public ToolError is CANCELLED at phase execute with outcome not_sent, verification none, safe_to_retry true, required_next_step none, details.downstream_stage write, and null public action counts; ordered harness evidence records one requested action, zero dispatched actions, zero completed actions, and zero BrowserPlane writes.

fault_script.action: Clear the one-shot keyboard cancellation injector and release the request and observation reservations.
fault_script.expected_effect: No later keyboard case inherits the cancellation fault; the same fresh observation remains available to the zero-dispatch validation cases.

fault_script.action: Cancel the two-action keyboard request after action 0 completes and action 1 is dispatched, but before action 1 completes.
fault_script.expected_effect: The public ToolError is CANCELLED with outcome unknown, failed_action_index 1, dispatched_action_count 2, completed_action_count 1, every action after index 1 suppressed, and ordinary held-state cleanup.

steps.expect: CANCELLED mutation ToolError with phase execute, outcome not_sent, verification none, safe_to_retry true, required_next_step none, details.failed_action_index null, details.dispatched_action_count null, details.completed_action_count null, and details.downstream_stage write. Separately, ordered harness evidence records requested_action_count 1, dispatched_action_count 0, completed_action_count 0, and exactly zero BrowserPlane writes.

Strict input-schema rejection before the keyboard handler, controller, adapter, or BrowserPlane call; ordered harness evidence records requested action index 0 and exactly zero BrowserPlane dispatches.

Strict input-schema rejection of the noncanonical physical key before the keyboard handler, controller, adapter, or BrowserPlane call; ordered harness evidence records requested action index 0 and exactly zero BrowserPlane dispatches.

INVALID_KEY mutation ToolError with phase validate, outcome not_sent, verification none, safe_to_retry false, required_next_step none, details.failed_action_index null, details.dispatched_action_count null, details.completed_action_count null, and details.downstream_stage none. Separately, controller evidence identifies rejected action index 0 with requested_action_count 1, dispatched_action_count 0, completed_action_count 0, and exactly zero BrowserPlane dispatches.

Applied mutation result with outcome applied, verification device_ack_only, safe_to_retry false, required_next_step none, dispatched_action_count 2, completed_action_count 2, and held_keys empty. BrowserPlane evidence is ControlLeft down, KeyA down, KeyA up, ControlLeft up, Enter down, Enter up in that exact order.
A second fresh unused observation for the faulted multi-action keyboard request.

CANCELLED mutation ToolError with phase execute, outcome unknown, verification none, safe_to_retry false, required_next_step inspect_device_state_before_retry, details.failed_action_index 1, details.dispatched_action_count 2, details.completed_action_count 1, and details.downstream_stage write. Counts are bounded by the two-action request, every action after index 1 is suppressed, and cleanup empties held_keys.

pass.assertion: The explicit pre-write cancellation call returns canonical CANCELLED/not_sent with null public counts, while separate ordered harness evidence records one requested action, zero dispatched and completed actions, and zero BrowserPlane writes.

The explicit two-action post-write cancellation call is unknown with failed_action_index equal to completed_action_count, dispatched_action_count equal to completed_action_count plus 1, the exact tuple 1/2/1, no dispatch after index 1, and empty held state after cleanup.

Executable text-action and noncanonical-key calls fail strict schema before the handler; executable key_up-without-held-state reaches the controller and returns INVALID_KEY/not_sent; all three record exactly zero BrowserPlane dispatches, while the valid chord and Enter sequence completes in exact physical order.

evidence.source: The linked focused assertion and ordered fake, replay, or live event record.

The focused keyboard count assertion and ordered fake or replay events proving request length 2, failed_action_index 1, dispatched_action_count 2, completed_action_count 1, and no dispatch after index 1.
The linked focused assertion and ordered fake, replay, or live event record.

restore.action: Invoke generation-correlated emergency input release when a session exists.
restore.assertion: No held key, button, wheel producer, macro, or paste producer remains.

restore.action: Stop and join any Reliable Paste producer.
restore.assertion: Reliable Paste is inactive and its correlated producer is joined.

restore.action: Verify the fixture reports zero held keys and buttons.
restore.assertion: Held keyboard and pointer state is empty.

restore.action: Close only the device session created by this story and its browser resources.
restore.assertion: No story-owned browser, channel, or device session remains.

restore.action: Clear deterministic faults and restore the recorded fixture baseline.
restore.assertion: The next story starts only after the safe baseline is verified.

privacy.rule: Persist only allowlisted opaque identifiers, sanitized versions, counts, durations, hashes, outcomes, verification, and restoration results.
